title: Petco 2025 Earnings and 2026 Guidance Summary
text: Petco 2025 Earnings and 2026 Guidance Summary. Full-Year 2025 Results: Petco reported mixed topline performance but strong profitability improvements. Net sales reached $6.0 billion, declining 2.5% year-over-year with comparable sales down 1.6%. However, the company demonstrated significant operational efficiency gains. Profitability Highlights: Adjusted EBITDA surged 21.3% to $408.2 million, exceeding company guidance. Net income improved dramatically to $9.1 million versus a $101.8 million loss in 2024. Gross margin expanded 66 basis points to 38.7%. Balance Sheet Strengthening: The company prioritized debt reduction and liquidity enhancement. Cash increased by $91 million to $256.7 million following a voluntary $95 million debt paydown. The leverage ratio improved significantly from 4.2x to 3.0x by year-end, demonstrating progress on deleveraging goals. Free cash flow surged 276.3% to $187.0 million, reflecting operational improvements and inventory optimization. The company also completed debt refinancing, extending maturities to 2031. 2026 Guidance: Management projects a return to positive comps in 2026, according to CEO Joel Anderson, indicating cautious optimism about topline recovery. Full-Year 2026 Outlook: Net sales guidance: flat to +1.5% year-over-year. Adjusted EBITDA: $415–$430 million. Planned net store closures: 15–20 locations. Capital expenditures: approximately $140 million. Q1 2026 Expectations: First quarter guidance signals near-term caution, projecting net sales down 1% to flat with adjusted EBITDA of $92–$94 million. The company assumes stable economic conditions, normalized fuel prices, and tariff levels remaining consistent with March 2026 levels.
